title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman set the MLB record for most strikeouts by a relief pitcher, reaching 1,364 on Friday night.
Chapman achieved the milestone with the Boston Red Sox against the Los Angeles Angels, recording the save in a 5-2 win.
Chapman, 38, surpassed Hall of Famer Hoyt Wilhelm's previous record, achieved in significantly fewer innings pitched.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranked as the most collision-prone city in the U.S. for the second year, with accidents every 3.7 years.
Worcester and Springfield, Massachusetts, were also among the bottom-ranked cities, with crashes roughly every 5 years.
Allstate's report uses insurance claims and app data, highlighting risky behaviors like phone use and speeding in Boston.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, was arrested after a 105 mph wrong-way chase over Bourne Bridge in Falmouth.
Deleon faces over a dozen charges including obstructing an emergency vehicle and assault with a dangerous weapon.
He is scheduled for a pretrial hearing on Aug. 5 after being held without bail due to dangerousness.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released surveillance photos of a second juvenile suspect in the armed robbery of a South Boston lemonade stand.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested and charged with armed robbery and unlawful possession of a firearm in the same case.
Police are soliciting information about the second suspect, with contact options for anonymous tips available.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Rich Paul lists 'Brad + Tatum' as a possible, but less likely, landing spot for LeBron James.
LeBron James is preparing to leave the Lakers and explore options for a fifth NBA Championship.
The Celtics are retooling their roster aggressively, already adding several players including Paul George.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticizes the Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish compares the trade to the Dallas Mavericks' regrettable decision to move Luka Doncic in 2025.
Parish expresses hope that Jaylen Brown and the Celtics will maintain a positive relationship despite the trade.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed gratitude to Jaylen Brown on social media after Brown's trade from the Celtics to the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded for Paul George and draft picks, reportedly feeling disrespected by the Celtics during the process.
Brown will play for the 76ers, facing his former team, the Celtics, four times in the 2026-27 season.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics extended Neemias Queta's contract for four years worth $56 million through the 2030-31 season.
Queta finished fourth in the 2025-26 Most Improved Player voting with averages of 10.2 points and 8.4 rebounds.
Boston picked up Queta’s 2026-27 team option amidst major roster changes, including trading Jaylen Brown to the 76ers.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant, located in Boston's Blackstone Block Historic District.
The restaurant's building dates back to 1716 or 1717, housing notable entities like Isaiah Thomas's 'The Massachusetts Spy' newspaper.
Union Oyster House serves 1,000 to 1,500 guests daily, selling over a million oysters and 10,000 gallons of clam chowder yearly.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston hosts major events including the Pops Fireworks, World Cup quarterfinal, and Sail Boston from July 4-11.
Gillette Stadium (Boston Stadium) will host a World Cup quarterfinal on July 9, impacting Route 1 traffic.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail starts July 11, with over 60 tall ships marking America's 250th anniversary.
